title: dryopteris serra var. augescens
intro: Dryopteris serra var. augescens, also known as Serrated Wood Fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower fever, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic due to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dryopteris serra var. augescens include its potential to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
